text: {worker}, a {worker_prof}, has stolen the company payroll ledger. It predicts every missing hour with supernatural accuracy. Management calls the arithmetic inflammatory.

outcomes.text: Copies spread before the ink dries. Workers discover they have all been individually experiencing the same coincidence.
effects: rumor 95 The payroll ledger proves systematic wage theft.

outcomes.text: They pay well for the return of their own handwriting. The missing wages remain missing, but your purse achieves closure.
One debt is paid quietly. The company thanks you for preserving the valuable fiction that this was an error.

text: Every tenant on Brasshook Street receives the same notice: rent has risen due to atmospheric market pressure. The sky denies involvement.

success.text: Thirty households discover that a landlord cannot evict a street without first locating thirty functional doors.
effects: rumor 90 Brasshook tenants have stopped paying together.

fail.text: The meeting is infiltrated by three property managers and a decorative fern wearing a wire.
effects: foreshadow 3 r15_eviction_morning Eviction crews are gathering beside Brasshook Street.

outcomes.text: The landlord accepts your money and promises not to become weather again until next month.
The desk, chair, and landlord experience the housing market from the pavement. The pavement lists several code violations.
effects: rumor 75 A landlord's office has become an outdoor public service.

text: The eviction crews arrive at dawn with hired guards, six wagons, and one clerk whose soul has been notarized.

choices.text: Build the barricade with everyone else

success.text: The street holds. By sunset, the furniture is still inside and the guards are discussing their own rent.

fail.text: The barricade breaks, but not before every child learns who ordered it broken.
effects: rumor 95 The Brasshook eviction was enforced with clubs.

outcomes.text: The clerk grants seven days because procedure enjoys postponing what courage might prevent.
effects: rumor 80 Brasshook tenants won one week and intend to use every hour.

choices.text: Identify the hired guards to their neighbors

outcomes.text: Half the guards leave when their mothers arrive. The other half discover masks are less effective against childhood nicknames.

text: Children downstream from the refinery collect luminous frogs. The refinery's physician explains that glowing is not listed as an illness.

choices.text: Take samples and publish the results

success.text: The poison has a signature, a source, and a board member who suddenly prefers anonymity.
effects: rumor 98 The refinery poisoned the downstream wetlands.

fail.text: The sample jar eats the label. Your eyebrows submit a separate report.

outcomes.text: Your study finds no causal relationship between poison and whoever paid for the study.
effects: rumor 15 Independent research has cleared the refinery.

choices.text: Help families block the discharge pipe

outcomes.text: The pipe stops. Production stops. Management discovers the environment had leverage after all.

text: The lampworkers have built a clean-power grid from mirrors, copper, and spite. The coal guild has filed a complaint against the sun.

choices.text: Defend the grid as public infrastructure

outcomes.text: Every roof joins the circuit. Nobody can meter the sun without standing in front of it looking ridiculous.
You own seventeen degrees of daylight. Dawn now arrives with licensing terms.
effects: rumor 35 Sunlight may require a subscription.

choices.text: Broker peace with the coal guild

outcomes.text: The guild keeps half its furnaces open and calls this a courageous transition. The air coughs politely.

text: A league in matching brown capes offers free soup, provided diners blame a minority for the empty pantry. Their sponsor owns three locked granaries.

choices.text: Open the granaries and label the sponsor

outcomes.text: Food reaches the square. The capes lose interest once hunger has an address and it is their donor's warehouse.
effects: foreshadow 4 r15_banner_march The brown-cape league is planning a public march.

outcomes.text: They use your civility as stage dressing, then print only the parts where you called them concerned citizens.
effects: rumor 20 The brown-cape league defeated its critics in open debate.

outcomes.text: The soup is warm. The price is being counted in a crowd photograph.

text: The brown-cape league marches behind respectable merchants, frightened officials, and a banner insisting hierarchy is merely traditional furniture.

success.text: The league finds every street occupied by people who know one another. Their courage depended on isolation.

fail.text: The counter-march is beaten back. It returns the next evening with medics, witnesses, and twice the number.
effects: rumor 95 The league attacked an unarmed counter-march.

choices.text: Ask officials to enforce their own laws

outcomes.text: Officials form a commission to determine whether marching toward violence constitutes movement.

success.text: The respectable money becomes publicly legible. Several capes remember urgent appointments elsewhere.

fail.text: You expose one shell company and accidentally become director of another.

text: A train of displaced families reaches a sealed border. The guard captain has orders to return them to people who have already published their graves.

choices.text: Open the gate and organize shelter

outcomes.text: The gate opens because enough hands pull at once. The city gains neighbors, recipes, testimony, and a furious border ministry.

choices.text: Follow orders to avoid a wider conflict

outcomes.text: The train reverses. Every official involved describes the outcome as regrettable and therefore nobody's fault.
effects: rumor 100 The border returned families to a documented extermination campaign.

choices.text: Forge transit papers for another destination

success.text: The papers are accepted because bureaucracy respects a confident stamp more than a living person.

fail.text: The ink is wrong. The families scatter while you occupy every guard at once.

text: Six deserters carry a map showing the offensive will cross three villages not mentioned in the general's speech.

choices.text: Help warn and evacuate the villages

outcomes.text: Carts leave before the army arrives. The general captures three wells, fourteen chickens, and nobody available to applaud.
effects: rumor 98 The planned offensive targeted inhabited villages.

outcomes.text: The army pays a bounty and executes the evidence. The map remains accurate.

choices.text: Leak the map to both armies

outcomes.text: Each side accuses the other of knowing where the villages are. Diplomacy briefly occurs by clerical accident.
effects: rumor 85 Soldiers on both sides know the offensive is a slaughter.

text: A war-bond gala serves tiny cakes shaped like conquered provinces. None of the guests can locate those provinces without eating them first.

choices.text: Read the casualty list during dessert

outcomes.text: The room objects to your tone, your timing, and eventually the names themselves.
effects: rumor 100 The war gala applauded until the dead were named.

choices.text: Sell bonds to the loudest patriots

outcomes.text: Patriotism proves highly liquid when repayment is assigned to unborn taxpayers.

choices.text: Replace the province cakes with invoices

outcomes.text: Several guests leave when asked to pay the actual cost of the evening.

text: The Council for Immediate Moderation has scheduled a listening session about whether the burning orphanage should be addressed before consensus is achieved.

outcomes.text: The children live. The council censures you for creating facts before all stakeholders could frame them.
The final resolution condemns fire in the strongest possible language. The orphanage is unavailable for comment.
effects: rumor 100 The council completed its statement after the orphanage burned.

choices.text: Move the session into the orphanage

outcomes.text: Urgency becomes bipartisan at approximately eyebrow temperature.

text: A private-equity necromancer buys the failing hospital, sells its beds, leases back the ghosts, and calls the screams operational improvement.

choices.text: Seize the books and reopen the wards

success.text: The debt was owed to a coffin company owned by the necromancer. The circle closes, then gets nationalized by the patients.

fail.text: You defeat three shell companies. A fourth shell company repossesses the shells.

outcomes.text: Your return is excellent. The hospital is now a premium ruin with optional oxygen.
The ghosts become less evil but significantly worse at filing.
effects: rumor 70 The hospital debts may have been invented after the patients arrived.

text: A refinery has purchased carbon offsets from an ogre who promises not to burn a forest he does not own. The forest continues dying from refinery sludge.

choices.text: Cancel the offset and stop the discharge

outcomes.text: Emissions fall immediately. The accounting department reports an unprecedented rise in reality.

choices.text: Sell the ogre a second imaginary forest

outcomes.text: You profit twice from protecting nothing. Finance calls this scale.

choices.text: Teach the ogre what an offset is

outcomes.text: The ogre becomes furious that humans invented fraud without even eating anyone.
effects: rumor 90 The refinery's forest offsets protect no actual forest.

text: A mine collapse traps twelve workers. The owner asks whether rescue costs can be deferred until ore prices improve.

success.text: All twelve emerge. The thirteenth person underground was an inspector hiding from the owner.

fail.text: Nine emerge. Nobody calls three deaths an acceptable margin except the owner, who does so repeatedly.
effects: rumor 100 The mine owner delayed rescue over ore prices.

choices.text: Wait for the owner's approved contractor

outcomes.text: The contractor arrives with branding, waivers, and no ropes.
The workers own a damaged mine and immediately make it safer than the profitable one.

text: A public clinic treats everyone for free. Its waiting room contains forty patients, two doctors, and a donation jar threatening to become fiscal policy.

choices.text: Spend the day helping the clinic

outcomes.text: You clean instruments, carry soup, and learn that care is mostly unglamorous tasks performed before anyone asks.
The queue shortens by excluding the people who needed it. Efficiency has excellent posture.

success.text: The funds were allocated to a ceremonial cannon. The cannon is retrained as an ambulance.

fail.text: The budget contains a corridor that returns to its own entrance.

text: A mutual-aid kitchen serves whoever arrives. Its inventory system is a grandmother pointing at the pantry and saying, 'We have enough if nobody gets ambitious.'

outcomes.text: Nobody names a wing after you. Several people eat. The pots remain politically unconvinced.

choices.text: Turn it into a branded charity

outcomes.text: Donations rise after meals shrink enough to fit beneath your logo.

choices.text: Take food and promise to return

outcomes.text: They feed you without auditing your promise. Trust is inefficient and somehow dinner exists.

text: A visitor from a post-scarcity federation repairs the town water system for free. The mayor demands an invoice so the gift can be made economically real.

choices.text: Explain that survival is real already

outcomes.text: The mayor stares at clean water, then at the blank invoice. One of these appears to frighten him.
The town pays you to describe the free repair. Civilization survives another billing cycle.

choices.text: Ask the visitor what humanity became

outcomes.text: Better, they say. Not pure. Better is work performed every morning, especially after victory.
effects: rumor 85 A future federation claims humanity eventually chose care over accumulation.

text: A damaged fabricator can produce unlimited bread. Its license server insists hunger is an unauthorized use case.

success.text: Bread appears without permission. Lawyers materialize more slowly and are distributed among the hungry.

fail.text: The machine produces a loaf containing the complete license agreement. It is dry and legally binding.

outcomes.text: Artificial scarcity is successfully restored. The machine congratulates you on preserving innovation.

choices.text: Give it to the public kitchen

outcomes.text: The kitchen becomes impossible to starve and correspondingly difficult to govern from above.

text: A pirate crew votes on whether captains are necessary. The captain argues yes with a prepared presentation titled 'Horizontal Command Structures and Me.'

outcomes.text: The crew keeps the navigation expertise and loses the decorative tyranny. The former captain is reassigned to rope.
effects: rumor 88 A pirate crew has made command recallable.

outcomes.text: The captain wins by one vote, having counted the ship as a dependent.
The ship receives thirty-two simultaneous orders and somehow docks perfectly out of mutual embarrassment.

text: The harbor is silent. Dockworkers demand safe cranes and back pay. Merchants call the strike violence against cargo.

choices.text: Join the picket and relief fund

outcomes.text: Ships wait. Families eat. On day three, the merchants discover negotiations were hidden inside the word no.

choices.text: Unload one ship for premium pay

outcomes.text: The money is excellent. Every crate is heavier when lifted under the names of people who trusted you.
Three cranes are unsafe. The fourth is two ladders expressing confidence.
effects: rumor 95 Harbor cranes were kept running after failed inspections.

text: The ministry nationalized the factory but retained the owner as Director of Revolutionary Continuity. Workers still need permission to use the bathroom.

outcomes.text: Production continues. The director's office becomes a break room, finally producing something useful.
The transition remains five years away, as it has for eighteen years. The posters are extremely liberated.

choices.text: Replace the director with a committee

outcomes.text: The committee has eleven members and one chair. The chair is the former director wearing a smaller hat.

text: A private guard company sells protection from unrest caused by the factory it also owns. Its brochure calls this vertical integration.

outcomes.text: The conflict of interest was perfectly legal and therefore becomes much harder to defend in public.
effects: rumor 98 The guard company and the abusive factory share an owner.

outcomes.text: The guards protect you from everyone unable to afford guards, which was the product all along.

choices.text: Help workers form their own safety patrol

outcomes.text: The patrol carries whistles, first-aid kits, and no obligation to manufacture fear for shareholders.

text: The Office of Public Reassurance has printed '{claim}' beneath a portrait of a minister looking accidentally haunted.

choices.text: Trace the claim to its source

outcomes.text: The source is a consultancy owned by the policy the rumor supports. The circle invoices itself.
effects: rumor 92 The reassurance office purchases rumors from interested parties.

choices.text: Improve the slogan for a fee

outcomes.text: Your version is shorter, warmer, and no more connected to reality.

choices.text: Paste witness accounts over every poster

outcomes.text: The city reads until the paste runs out. Reassurance becomes significantly less reassuring.

text: {candidate} is running in a union election. Their opponent promises labor peace, employer donations, and a much quieter union.

outcomes.text: Members stop asking which candidate will save them and begin asking what they can decide together.
Labor peace arrives. It resembles management speaking without interruption.
The meeting fractures into seven correct minorities. Management wins unanimously without attending.

text: A caravan carries replacement workers toward a strike. Most were told the job was ordinary. The recruiter was told the lie was efficient.

choices.text: Tell the workers what they are entering

outcomes.text: Half turn back. The rest ask for the strike address so they can join it properly.
effects: rumor 90 Replacement workers were recruited under false pretenses.

choices.text: Rob the recruiter and fund the strike

outcomes.text: The recruiter reports wage theft with a sudden and beautiful understanding of the term.
The recruiter pays per delivered conscience. Yours receives the standard rate.

text: A seawall protects the wealthy quarter by directing storm water into the workers' district. Engineers call the flooding a successful displacement of risk.

choices.text: Redesign protection for the whole coast

success.text: The new wall protects homes rather than property values. Several investors feel personally attacked by geometry.

fail.text: The tide respects neither your calculations nor the deed registry.

outcomes.text: You become landlord of six wet rooms and one extremely unionized eel.
The storm distributes itself more equally. This is not justice, but it gets the investors to attend the next meeting.

text: An overcrowded boat signals for help. The port authority warns that rescue may encourage people to continue not drowning.

outcomes.text: The deck becomes crowded, frightened, alive, and therefore a success by every measure not designed to avoid responsibility.
Authorization arrives for a boat that no longer exists. The stamp is perfectly dry.
effects: rumor 100 A rescue was delayed until there was nobody left to rescue.

choices.text: Tow them beyond the authority's jurisdiction

outcomes.text: Human life becomes legal again twelve nautical miles east.

text: A new fence surrounds the village commons. The deed claims the land belongs to a duke whose strongest connection to it is handwriting.

choices.text: Remove the fence with the village

outcomes.text: The fence becomes firewood. The deed becomes kindling. The land remains stubbornly under everybody.
The duke sells permission for grass to continue existing beneath cows.

choices.text: Claim to be a second duke

outcomes.text: Your handwriting is larger. Property law enters a constitutional crisis.

text: Veterans of a failed uprising hold a picnic where the barricade once stood. They argue about mistakes while passing bread to everyone, including the people they blame.

choices.text: Ask what the failure taught them

outcomes.text: They list bad timing, isolated neighborhoods, heroic vanity, and not enough soup. The next attempt already has a kitchen committee.
effects: rumor 85 The failed uprising left behind organizers, not only graves.

outcomes.text: An old woman agrees that failure is painful, then asks why your conclusion resembles the ruler's favorite lesson.
History does not move. The square becomes clean, the names become readable, and tomorrow's meeting has chairs.

text: An auditor asks why you helped a stranger without documenting expected returns. The form has no box for because they needed help.

choices.text: Write that answer across the whole form

outcomes.text: The form is rejected. The sentence survives in the office, copied by clerks who have needed help before.
effects: rumor 80 Someone has introduced need as a reason for public action.

outcomes.text: Compassion futures rise sharply. Actual compassion becomes too expensive for ordinary use.
They leave with a stolen stapler and the expression of someone meeting their own afternoon for the first time.
